seoTitle: Add and Manage Tabs in Smartsheet Easily
metaDescription: Manage multiple open sheets in Smartsheet using the tabbed navigation bar, the desktop app's multi-window docking, and quick keyboard shortcuts.
intro: Juggling several project sheets at once can get messy fast. Smartsheet's tabbed navigation, especially through the desktop app, lets you keep multiple sheets, reports, and dashboards open side by side. This guide covers opening tabs, pinning important ones, and switching between them quickly.

steps.heading: Open multiple sheets to populate the tab bar
steps.body: Open several sheets, reports, or dashboards at once to generate a tabbed navigation bar at the top of your screen.

steps.heading: Use the desktop app for more control
steps.body: The Smartsheet Desktop App unlocks standalone tab controls and multi-window docking that the browser version doesn't offer.

steps.heading: Pin or manage tabs
steps.body: Right-click an active tab to pin critical files, clone tab layouts, or close background tabs quickly.

steps.heading: Switch tabs with keyboard shortcuts
steps.body: Use Ctrl+Tab or Cmd+Tab to move between open sheets instantly without reaching for the mouse.

steps.heading: Keep your workspace organized
steps.body: Group related project timelines into workspace folders and dashboards so your open tabs stay manageable.

faq.question: Can I pin a tab so it doesn't close accidentally?
faq.answer: Yes, right-clicking an active tab lets you pin it so it stays open alongside your other work.

faq.question: Is there a keyboard shortcut to switch between sheets?
faq.answer: Yes, Ctrl+Tab on Windows or Cmd+Tab on Mac lets you cycle between open sheets.

faq.question: Does the desktop app offer more tab features than the browser?
faq.answer: Yes, the Smartsheet Desktop App adds standalone tab controls and multi-window docking capabilities.

faq.question: Will my filters stay applied when I switch tabs?
faq.answer: Preserving active filters when switching views is something to check, since layout shifts can occasionally affect them.
